info.name: API Security Audit - Future Interns 2026
info.description:
Read-only API Security Risk Analysis collection for testing JSONPlaceholder and ReqRes APIs.

⚠ ETHICAL SCOPE: Read-only testing only. No exploitation. No DoS testing.
Framework: OWASP API Security Top 10 (2023)

item.name: F1 - GET All Users (No Auth Required)

request.description:
FINDING F1 | Severity: HIGH | OWASP: API2:2023

Test: Can we retrieve all users with NO authentication?
Expected (secure): HTTP 401 Unauthorized
Actual: HTTP 200 OK — Full user list returned with no token required.

Risk: Any anonymous internet user can access the complete user database including names, emails, phone numbers, addresses, and geo-coordinates.

item.name: F2 - GET User by ID (BOLA - User 1)

request.description:
FINDING F2 | Severity: HIGH | OWASP: API1:2023

Test: Can we access User 1's data with no ownership check?
Expected (secure): HTTP 403 if requesting user is not User 1
Actual: HTTP 200 — Complete user profile returned.

Risk: BOLA vulnerability. Change ID to /users/2, /users/3 etc. to access any user.

item.name: F2 - GET User by ID (BOLA - User 2)

request.description: FINDING F2 CONTINUED | Enumeration test — change ID from 1 to 2 to access a different user's data. No error returned.

FINDING F3 | Severity: HIGH | OWASP: API2:2023

Test: Can we create data with NO authentication?
Expected (secure): HTTP 401 Unauthorized
Actual: HTTP 201 Created — Data accepted with no token.

Risk: Anyone can create, spam, or inject data without logging in.

item.name: F4 - Rate Limit Test (Send Multiple Times)

request.description:
FINDING F4 | Severity: MEDIUM | OWASP: API4:2023

Test: Does the API block or throttle repeated rapid requests?
Instructions: Click Send 20+ times rapidly (or use Postman Runner)
Expected (secure): HTTP 429 Too Many Requests after threshold
Actual: HTTP 200 every time — No rate limiting present.

Look for headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, Retry-After
Result: NONE of these headers present.

FINDING F5 | Severity: MEDIUM | OWASP: API3:2023

Test: How many fields does the API return? Are all of them necessary?

Count the fields in the response:
- id, name, username, email (4 — potentially needed)
- address.street, .suite, .city, .zipcode (4 — usually not needed in API responses)
- address.geo.lat, .geo.lng (2 — privacy risk: physical location)
- phone, website (2 — often unnecessary)
- company.name, .catchPhrase, .bs (3 — almost never needed)

Total: 15+ fields returned when most use cases need 2-3.
Risk: Excessive data exposure increases breach impact.

FINDING F7 | Severity: LOW | OWASP: API8:2023

After sending this request, click the 'Headers' tab in the Response panel.

Check for MISSING security headers:
❌ X-Content-Type-Options (should be: nosniff)
❌ X-Frame-Options (should be: DENY)
❌ Content-Security-Policy (should be present)
❌ Strict-Transport-Security (should be present)
❌ X-RateLimit-* headers (should be present)
❌ Permissions-Policy (should be present)

These are all missing = Security Misconfiguration finding.

Setup: Get authentication token from ReqRes login.

Note the 'token' value in the response — save it.
This token SHOULD be required for subsequent requests.
Finding F6 tests whether the token is actually enforced.

item.name: F6 - GET Users WITHOUT Token (Should Fail)

request.description:
FINDING F6 | Severity: MEDIUM | OWASP: API2:2023

Test: Does the /api/users endpoint require the auth token we got from login?
Expected (secure): HTTP 401 Unauthorized (token required)
Actual: HTTP 200 OK — Full user list returned WITHOUT any token.

Risk: The login system issues tokens but they are never actually enforced.
The authentication mechanism is cosmetic — any unauthenticated request succeeds.

header.description: Token from login response — replace with actual token

request.description:
Comparison test: Send the same request WITH the auth token.

Both the authenticated and unauthenticated requests return HTTP 200.
This confirms the token is not being validated server-side.
The presence or absence of the token makes no difference.

FINDING F7 (continued) | Severity: LOW | OWASP: API8:2023

Check response headers for the same missing security headers as JSONPlaceholder.
Verify both APIs share this finding.
